content:
You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>in chuck/BI trovi un file che devo compilare mensilmente. trovi tutte le informazioni in https://beoneanalytics.novasystems.it/hub/my/favorites nel pannello finance dove, all'interno, trovi Bilancio. aprendo i vari menù a tendina nei totals (es costi produzione-22-02 - 22-02-01 000003 energ elett acqua e gas) trovi le varie voci. inizia leggere il bilancio e cerca di compilare il foglio. in caso mancassero delle voci, dimmi quali.</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-03-11</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING START
=======================

The user is asking me to observe a Claude Code session and create observations about what's being done. Let me analyze the user request:

The user wants to:
1. Find a file in `chuck/BI` that needs to be compiled monthly
2. Get information from https://beoneanalytics.novasystems.it/hub/my/favorites
3. Navigate to the "finance" panel and then "Bilancio" (Balance Sheet in Italian)
4. Open dropdown menus in totals (e.g., "costi produzione-22-02 - 22-02-01 000003 energ elett acqua e gas")
5. Read the balance sheet data and compile a spreadsheet
6. Report any missing entries

This appears to be a task about:
- Accessing a business intelligence system (BeOne Analytics)
- Reading financial/accounting data (Bilancio = Balance Sheet)
- Filling out a monthly reporting file
- The file is located in chuck/BI directory
- Data is from a web-based system

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>mcp__plugin_playwright_playwright__browser_evaluate</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-03-11T14:36:02.387Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/BI</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"function\":\"() => {\\n  // Find all scrollable containers in Qlik Sense\\n  const allElements = document.querySelectorAll('*');\\n  const scrollable = [];\\n  for (let el of allElements) {\\n    if (el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight + 10 && el.clientHeight > 100) {\\n      scrollable.push({\\n        tag: el.tagName,\\n        class: el.className.toString().substring(0, 80),\\n        scrollHeight: el.scrollHeight,\\n        clientHeight: el.clientHeight\\n      });\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return JSON.stringify(scrollable.slice(0, 10));\\n}\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"### Result\\n\\\"[{\\\\\\\"tag\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"DIV\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"class\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"ListBox-styledScrollbars css-1nwu5vb\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"scrollHeight\\\\\\\":1131,\\\\\\\"clientHeight\\\\\\\":638},{\\\\\\\"tag\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"DIV\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"class\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"qv-grid-object-scroll-area ng-isolate-scope\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"scrollHeight\\\\\\\":986,\\\\\\\"clientHeight\\\\\\\":894}]\\\"\\n### Ran Playwright code\\n```js\\nawait page.evaluate('() => {\\\\n  // Find all scrollable containers in Qlik Sense\\\\n  const allElements = document.querySelectorAll(\\\\'*\\\\');\\\\n  const scrollable = [];\\\\n  for (let el of allElements) {\\\\n    if (el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight + 10 && el.clientHeight > 100) {\\\\n      scrollable.push({\\\\n        tag: el.tagName,\\\\n        class: el.className.toString().substring(0, 80),\\\\n        scrollHeight: el.scrollHeight,\\\\n        clientHeight: el.clientHeight\\\\n      });\\\\n    }\\\\n  }\\\\n  return JSON.stringify(scrollable.slice(0, 10));\\\\n}');\\n```\"}]"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
Now we have actual work being done. The session is using Playwright to interact with a Qlik Sense web application. They're:

1. Finding scrollable containers in the Qlik Sense interface
2. Looking for elements with scrollHeight > clientHeight (meaning they're scrollable)
3. Found 2 scrollable elements:
   - A ListBox with styled scrollbars (scrollHeight: 1131px, clientHeight: 638px)
   - A grid object scroll area (scrollHeight: 986px, clientHeight: 894px)

This is part of automating the access to the BeOne Analytics system mentioned in the user request. They're trying to navigate and interact with the financial data (Bilancio) in the web interface.

This is a discovery - they're learning about the structure of the Qlik Sense interface to automate navigation.

Let me create an observation for this.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Qlik Sense scrollable containers identified for data extraction</title>
  <subtitle>Browser automation discovered two scrollable elements in BeOne Analytics interface for navigating financial data</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Playwright browser evaluation executed to find scrollable containers in Qlik Sense application</fact>
    <fact>ListBox with class 'ListBox-styledScrollbars' found with scrollHeight 1131px and clientHeight 638px</fact>
    <fact>Grid object scroll area with class 'qv-grid-object-scroll-area' found with scrollHeight 986px and clientHeight 894px</fact>
    <fact>Working directory is /Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/BI for monthly financial compilation task</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Browser automation setup initiated to access BeOne Analytics (beoneanalytics.novasystems.it) for monthly financial reporting. The system uses Qlik Sense for displaying financial data (Bilancio). Playwright evaluation discovered scrollable containers in the interface, identifying two key elements: a ListBox component for navigation/selection and a grid object for data display. These scrollable elements will enable automated navigation through dropdown menus in the finance panel to extract balance sheet data for compiling the monthly reporting file located in chuck/BI directory.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
